ab: kusian a saikin asang, masa takna tantungu sian inaak tu tamahudas.
ch: 昨天，我去部落拜訪爺爺。
en: Yesterday, I went to visit my grandpa in the tribe.

ab: malbuntuin a kaimin nitu mapasadu, paha mililiskin a saikin mas saicia.
ch: 我們很久沒有見面了，我很想念他。
en: We haven’t seen each other for a long time and I miss him a lot.

ab: masa usaduan a tamahudas mas zaku hai, mapakaunku mas suika, mapahuudku mas ucia.
ch: 爺爺一看到我，就請我吃西瓜，請我喝茶。
en: As soon as grandpa saw me, he invited me to eat watermelon and drink tea.

ab: madavus a itutamahudas sinsuaza suika, tuza tu masial kaunun.
ch: 爺爺種的西瓜很甜，真的很好吃。
en: The watermelon grown by grandpa is very sweet and really delicious.

ab: mazima’ik amin mas itutamahudas tu ucia, masial amin huudan.
ch: 我也喜歡爺爺的茶，也很好喝。
en: I also like grandpa’s tea, it’s tasted good too.

ab: masa taunghuvali’in, muskunik mas cinahudas ma’anat mas pinittaunghuvali.
ch: 中午的時候，我和奶奶一起煮午餐。
en: At noon, I cooked lunch with my grandma.

ab: ma’anat a cinahudas mas vaivivaivi tu kakaunun, aiza mai’iskaan, aiza maitulkuk, aiza baat, aiza’ang maimumuan
ch: 奶奶煮了很豐盛的午餐，有魚、有雞、有南瓜、還有蝸牛。
en: Grandma cooked a big lunch, including fish, chicken, pumpkin, and snails.

ab: masa minaunin mas pinittaunghuvali hai, kusian kaimin pissanglavan kasanglav.
ch: 吃完午餐後，我們去菜園摘菜。
en: After lunch, we went to the vegetable garden to pick vegetables.

ab: siza a cinahudas mas supah tu utan, tai, cilkung, isaiv mas zaku tu na’ispa’adas kulumah.
ch: 奶奶拿了很多地瓜、芋頭和蕗蕎給我，要我帶回家。
en: Grandma brought me a lot of sweet potatoes, taro and buckwheat, and asked me to take them home.

ab: nasampalavaazin a kaimin at mapatupa tu, namuhnang tantungu maisinin.
ch: 我跟他們道別，說下次再去看他們。
en: I said goodbye to them and said I’d see them next time.
